CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT, name VARCHAR, age INT);
INSERT INTO test_table VALUES (3, NULL, NULL);
SELECT coalesce(age, 18) AS age_with_default FROM test_table;
SELECT id, coalesce(name, 'Anonymous', 'Guest') AS display_name FROM test_table;
SELECT id, coalesce(NULL, name, 'Fallback') AS name_or_fallback FROM test_table;
SELECT coalesce((SELECT name FROM test_table WHERE id = 1), 'Default Name') AS name_fallback;
SELECT id, coalesce(name, (SELECT name FROM test_table WHERE id = 2), 'No Name') AS name_or_default FROM test_table;
CREATE TABLE info (user_id INT, email VARCHAR);
SELECT a.id, coalesce(b.email, 'not_provided') AS user_email FROM test_table a LEFT JOIN info b ON a.id = b.user_id;
SELECT coalesce((SELECT MAX(age) FROM test_table WHERE age IS NOT NULL), 0) AS max_age;
SELECT coalesce((SELECT SUM(age) FROM test_table WHERE age IS NOT NULL), 'No ages provided') AS sum_age;
SELECT coalesce(NULL, NULL, (SELECT MIN(age) FROM test_table WHERE age IS NOT NULL), 0) AS min_age;
